pi_items.source: Explicitly required in task description and verifier.py logic.

The global bounds maxlat in fells_loop.gpx is exactly 42.468655. The claimed metadata contradicts the data provided in the environment.
The global bounds minlat in fells_loop.gpx is exactly 42.401051. The claimed metadata contradicts the data provided in the environment.
The global bounds maxlon in fells_loop.gpx is exactly -71.102973. The claimed metadata (-71.0921) is actually further east than any point in the provided dataset.
The global bounds minlon in fells_loop.gpx is exactly -71.126602. The claimed metadata contradicts the data provided in the environment.
Calculated the real NS span from the fells_loop.gpx bounding latitudes using the Haversine formula (approx 7.52 km). The claimed metadata contradicts the data provided.
Calculated the real EW span from the fells_loop.gpx bounding longitudes using the Haversine formula (approx 1.94 km). The claimed metadata contradicts the data provided.

privileged_info_summary: Completed PI audit for garmin_basecamp_env create_track_bounding_box task. Confirmed that the fells_loop.gpx dataset is a real sample from TopoGrafix corresponding to the Middlesex Fells Reservation. Discovered that the claimed ground truth bounds (N: 42.4485, S: 42.4343, E: -71.0921, W: -71.1147) completely contradict the true bounding box of the data provided in the environment (N: 42.468655, S: 42.401051, E: -71.102973, W: -71.126602). This means the task verifier checks against fabricated values that do not match the data. Additionally, a critical defect was identified: 'setup_task.ps1' and 'export_result.ps1' are entirely missing from the environment, which will cause the task setup and verification process to fail programmatically.
